zh.lifeSummary: Roger Savage於1396年出生在英國英格蘭爾克里夫頓。他於1442年與Elizabeth D'arcy在英國爾克里夫頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世在英國爾克里夫頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Roger Savage于1396年出生在英国英格兰爾克里夫頓。他于1442年与Elizabeth D'arcy在英国爾克里夫頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世在英国爾克里夫頓。

nameMeanings.name: Savage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Roger Savage was born in 1396, in Clifton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. He married Elizabeth D'arcy in 1442, in Clifton, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in Clifton, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Roger Savage est né en 1396, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Elizabeth D'arcy en 1442, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Roger Savage wurde 1396 in Clifton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1442 in Clifton, Cheshire, England Elizabeth D'arcy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb in Clifton, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Roger Savage è nato il nel 1396 nel Clifton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Elizabeth D'arcy nel 1442, nel Clifton, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel Clifton, Cheshire, England.

ja.Clifton, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアクリフトン
ja.lifeSummary: Roger Savageは1396年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンで生まれた。1442年にイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンでElizabeth D'arcyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Savage(은)는 1396년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 클리프턴에서 태어났다. 그는 1442년 영국 체실르 클리프턴에서 Elizabeth D'arcy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 체실르 클리프턴에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Roger Savage nasceu em 1396, em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth D'arcy em 1442, em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Roger Savage родился в 1396 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth D'arcy в 1442 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Roger Savage nació en 1396, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Elizabeth D'arcy en 1442, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
